data.title: Blue Owl affiliates to acquire Sila Realty Trust in ~$2.4B all-cash deal
data.description:
Sila Realty Trust (SILA) stock was surging after the net lease REIT announced a definitive merger agreement with certain affiliates of Blue Owl Capital (OWL) involving an all-cash transaction valued at ~$2.4B.

Affiliates of Blue Owl Real Estate Capital are set to buy all outstanding shares of Sila Realty Trust for $30.38 per share.

SILA shares were 18.92% higher to $30.36 Monday pre-market, while OWL was trading 1.12% lower at $9.74.

The purchase price represents a 19.0% premium to Sila's April 17 closing stock price of $25.53 and a 25.6% premium to the stock's 30-trading day volume weighted average price.

Both boards have unanimously approved the transaction, expected to close in the second or third quarter of 2026.

During the pendency of the transaction, Sila intends to pay up to two regular quarterly dividends. 

The Tampa, Florida-based company said it does not expect to host a conference call and webcast to discuss its financial results for the quarter ended March 31.

data.title: Onto Innovation rises as Needham ups price target after customer win
data.description:
• Onto Innovation (ONTO) shares rose around 1% in premarket trading on Monday as Needham upped its price target on the stock to $320 from $275 after it disclosed a major customer win and pre-announced results last week.
  • “On April 15th, Onto Innovation announced a leading foundry customer has qualified its DragonFly G5 product,” analyst Charles Shi wrote in a note to clients. “Along with the product news, the company pre-announced its 1Q26 results and guided 2Q26 above the Street, with upside in both quarters driven by a combination of advanced nodes and advanced packaging (including small contribution from G5). The company further noted that the advanced packaging revenue upside is from both foundry (e.g. TSMC) and OSAT (e.g. Amkor and ASE) customers.”

data.title: Arcus halts Gilead-partnered late-stage trial for lung cancer drug
data.description:
Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) traded lower in the premarket on Monday after the oncology-focused biotech announced the discontinuation of a late-stage trial designed to evaluate its anti-TIGIT therapy domvanalimab in lung cancer as part of a collaboration with Gilead Sciences (GILD).

In a regulatory filing on Monday, the Hayward, California-based biotech stated that the decision to halt the STAR-121 trial was taken due to futility, following the recommendation of its Independent Data Monitoring Committee.

STAR-121 evaluated domvanalimab with chemotherapy and anti-PD-1 antibody zimberelimab as a first-line option for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer versus chemotherapy and pembrolizumab, an anti-PD-1 drug Merck (MRK) markets as Keytruda.

While the IDRC’s review didn’t include safety data, its regular reviews didn’t identify any safety concerns related to domvanalimab, Arcus (RCUS) said.

Concurrently, the company announced the discontinuation of its Phase 2 EDGE-Lung study, which evaluated domvanalimab with various immunotherapies in patients with lung cancer.

Additionally, Arcus (RCUS) disclosed that effective July 14, 2026, Gilead (GILD) will no longer have option rights to certain programs in its early-stage pipeline, as the pharma giant has chosen not to pay the option continuation payment to the company.

data.title: Avis Budget is the most overbought industrials stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the industrials sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 industrials stocks ranked based on their Relative Strength Index.

The list is topped by Avis Budget Group (CAR), with an RSI of 93. GATX Corporation (GATX) and RXO, Inc. (RXO) are next, with Firefly Aerospace Inc. (FLY) and Legence Corp. (LGN) rounding out the rest of the top five. Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ), ArcBest Corporation (ARCB), and Griffon Corporation (GFF) are among the top ranked stocks as well, each with an RSI of 75 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. Avis Budget Group, Inc. (CAR), RSI: 93

  2. GATX Corporation (GATX), RSI: 78

  3. RXO, Inc. (RXO), RSI: 77

  4. Firefly Aerospace Inc. (FLY), RSI: 76

  5. Legence Corp. (LGN), RSI: 76

  6. Hertz Global Holdings, Inc. (HTZ), RSI: 76

  7. ArcBest Corporation (ARCB), RSI: 76

  8. Griffon Corporation (GFF), RSI: 75

  9. Andersen Group Inc. (ANDG), RSI: 75

  10. OPENLANE, Inc. (OPLN), RSI: 74

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)

data.title: Silvaco partners with ITRI to support MCU development and startup innovation
data.description:
• Silvaco Group (SVCO) announced on Monday a strategic partnership with the Industrial Technology Research Institute ((ITRI)).
  • Under this collaboration, ITRI will adopt Silvaco's SmartSpice circuit simulation solution, supporting ITRI’s advanced microcontroller unit (MCU) development and startup acceleration initiatives.
  • ITRI, an R&D institution based in Taiwan, operates the Nankang IC Design Incubation Center, which fosters innovation in semiconductor technology and supports emerging companies in bringing breakthrough technologies to market.
  • Through this partnership, ITRI has deployed Silvaco's SmartSpice platform within the incubator to enable precise circuit simulation and verification for next-generation MCU designs developed by startups within the incubator program.
  • Silvaco and ITRI are exploring additional partnership and collaboration opportunities to support startup success. The incubator center provides comprehensive support to emerging semiconductor companies, combining office space, technical resources, R&D support, and business development guidance.
  • SVCO -1.29% premarket to $8.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jersey Mike's aims to be one of the ten most valuable restaurant companies with its IPO
data.description:
Jersey Mike's is looking to raise $1B at a valuation of at least $12 billion with its IPO, according to Bloomberg. The restaurant company is in talks with underwriters about details on the size of its offering. Jersey Mike's submitted a confidential filing with the SEC as it prepares for the IPO.

Sources indicate that the Blackstone (BX)-backed company is seeking to complete the IPO as soon as Q3 of this year.

Jersey Mike's traces its roots to 1956, when the original Mike’s Subs opened in Point Pleasant, New Jersey. Founder Peter Cancro bought that store and began to franchise the chain in the late 1980s. From the original single store, Jersey Mike’s has scaled to operate over 3,200 sandwich shops across the U.S. and Canada. It is largely recognized as one of the fastest-growing fast-casual brands. Importantly, consumer surveys point to strong name recognition and favorability for the chain.

Looking ahead, Jersey Mike’s has announced a major international push, signing a franchise agreement to open 400 stores in the United Kingdom and Ireland as part of a broader global expansion strategy. A Jersey Mike's IPO could help fund the international expansion plans while providing investors exposure to a scaled franchisor model with recurring royalty streams and asset-light economics.

Jersey Mike's competes directly with Jimmy John’s, Firehouse Subs, Potbelly (PBPB), Quiznos, and Penn Station East Coast Subs. On a more indirect basis, Jersey Mike's scraps for lunch traffic with Panera (PANERA), Portillo's (PTLO), McDonald's (MCD), Wendy's (WEN), and Burger King (QSR).

A $12B valuation would make Jersey Mike's the 9th largest restaurant company in the U.S. by market cap, just ahead of CAVA Group (CAVA), Texas Roadhouse (TXRH), and Dutch Bros (BROS).

data.title: Lionsgate is fairly priced for a potential acquisition—Citi Research
data.description:
While Lionsgate (LION) remains an attractive acquisition target to Citi Research analyst Jason Bazinet, the stock’s value already reflects an M&A premium, leading Bazinet to downgrade the stock to Neutral from Buy as he no longer finds the risk/reward “compelling.” 

Bazinet sees a scenario in which Lionsgate (LION) could be sold for three reasons. First, the separation of Lionsgate (LION) from Starz (STRZ) creates a pure-play studio. Second, at the Starz-Lionsgate separation, the board collapsed the dual-class share structure, making an acquisition easier. And third, in the latest proxy, Lionsgate (LION) management emphasized “strategic optionality” as a key priority.

So, given this scenario and the company’s debt, Bazinet assumes an acquisition multiple of ~18x 2027 EV/EBITDA, which translates into an acquisition price of $13.50 per share.

Citi’s prior target price of $11 per share reflected a 45% likelihood that the company would be sold for $13.50. Therefore, the firm is raising its target price to $12.60 to reflect a 75% likelihood that Lionsgate (LION) is sold for $13.50. On a standalone basis, the stock is worth $9 per share.

data.title: Salesforce CEO says SaaS not dead as AI creates opportunities: report
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) CEO and founder Marc Benioff has watched his company's stock fall 30% year-to-date as the noise about artificial intelligence disrupting SaaS has dragged down the entire sector.

The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has dropped 20% in that time frame.

However, Salesforce pivoted quickly to embrace the technology and use it to better serve its client base. It invested in Anthropic (ANTHRO) back in 2023, and it utilizes models from OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic to support its Agentforce offering. 

"People think we have our back against the wall when in fact the opportunity has never been greater," Benioff said in an interview with the Wall Street Journal.

Now, Salesforce is working on another AI platform, code-named Agent Albert, the report said. It studies users behaviors and can take actions for them. It follows up on Agentforce, which is being used by 23,000 of its 150,000-strong customer base.

Chicago Capital partner Mike Kimbarovsky, whose firm is a Salesforce investor, told the WSJ that the company needs "to show revolutionary jumps." While Agentforce has proven capable of handling customer issues related to order statuses, refunds, lost access codes, etc., it can have issues with more complex tasks that still require human intervention.

The seat-based model for Salesforce has also proved problematic for investors. If it stands to reason that AI applications can help companies do more work with fewer employees, then those same companies might eventually need fewer seats.

To help with this, Salesforce introduced a hybrid pricing model about a year ago, the report said. While customers can still purchase seat licenses, they pay more for consumption related to Agentforce through a price-per-action model.

What's more, Benioff said even if clients build their own customer management software with OpenAI or Anthropic, those programs can't replace the data security and industry-specific compliance that Salesforce and other SaaS companies have spent years building.

data.title: BRC Group to consolidate financial services platform
data.description:
BRC Group (RILY) on Monday announced a series of strategic actions to consolidate its financial services platform.

The diversified holding company intends to repurchase the outstanding shares of B. Riley Securities, and combine the unit with B. Riley Wealth. The merger is expected to be effective by the end of 2026.

The consolidation is expected to bring the Los Angeles-based company's institutional and retail distribution under a single investment banking and wealth management platform.

"To more efficiently deploy our combined capital resources and continue to accelerate growth of the platform, BRCGH (the group) will pursue a repurchase of the approximately 8% of minority shares of BRS (B. Riley Securities) not already owned by BRCGH," said Co-CEO Bryant Riley.

Bryant Riley has been appointed executive chairman of B. Riley Securities; Andy Moore as CEO; and Joseph Nardini as president, effective immediately.

Jimmy Baker has stepped down from his role as CEO of B. Riley Securities.

"These changes are focused on renewed growth and reinforce our commitment to BRS's long-term success," said BRS CEO Andy Moore.

Shares were 0.73% lower to $7.74.

data.title: Farmers & Merchants Bank of Long Beach GAAP EPS of $157.45, revenue of $81.98M
data.description:
• Farmers & Merchants Bank of Long Beach press release (FMBL): Q1 GAAP EPS of $157.45.
  • Revenue of $81.98M.

  • Gross loans were $6.34 billion as of March 31, 2026, compared with $6.08 billion as of December 31, 2025. The Bank’s allowance for loan losses totaled $85.2 million, or 1.35% of loans held-for-investment at March 31, 2026, compared with $84.0 million, or 1.39% of loans held-for investment at December 31, 2025.

    F&M’s total deposits at March 31, 2026 amounted to $8.94 billion, compared with $8.95 billion at December 31, 2025. Noninterest-bearing deposits represented 34.97% of total deposits at March 31, 2026, and 34.58% at December 31, 2025.

    Securities sold under repurchase agreements increased to $962.0 million at March 31, 2026, from $883.2 million at December 31, 2025. Borrowings were $300 million at March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025.

    Total assets at March 31, 2026, were $11.86 billion, compared with $11.80 billion at December 31, 2025. Total stockholders’ equity was $1.61 billion at March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025.

    Capital

    Capital ratios are the foundation of safety in all banks, and F&M continues to exceed most national and regional banks in this area, as well as exceeding regulatory capital requirements to meet the definition of a “well-capitalized” financial institution. As of March 31, 2026, the Bank’s total risk-based capital ratio was 21.94%; tier 1 risk-based capital ratio was 20.69%; common equity tier 1 capital ratio was 18.23%; and tier 1 leverage ratio was 13.61%. The minimum capital adequacy ratios for a “well-capitalized” bank are 10.00%, 8.00%, 6.50% and 5.00%, respectively.
